behavior_summary: This script implements two item event handlers for a trowel: the 'U8P_TROWEL::look' handler (U8P_TROWEL::look) and a 'use' handler (U8P_TROWEL::use).
U8P_TROWEL::look simply presents the item name/description by invoking Item::bark with the literal "trowel ", directing that message to the process/context that triggered the U8P_TROWEL::look.

U8P_TROWEL::use prompts the actor for a target repeatedly until one is provided, validates reachability with Item::canReach (barking "can't reach " and aborting if unreachable), accepts only targets of numeric type 3 or 4, calls FREE::24A7 as a gating precondition, and—on success—mutates the chosen target (sets quantity to 1, ORs status bit 0x0080) and causes the item to update/fall so the world state refreshes.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TROWEL::look: linear handler — initialize context and call Item::bark then return.
U8P_TROWEL::use: loops on process target() until a non-zero target id is returned, then performs a sequence of guards (canReach, type check, FREE::24A7) that can each cause an early return.
FREE::24A7 acts as a gate: only when it indicates success (inferred by a specific return value check) does the code proceed to mutate the target and call Item::fall.
open_questions: What exactly does FREE::24A7 check or allocate? (inferred as resource/permission check; semantics unknown).
What gameplay semantics correspond to numeric target types 3 and 4 (tile, creature, container, etc.)?
What is the gameplay meaning of setting the status bit 0x0080 on the target (inferred as a state change such as 'dug', 'marked', or 'processed')?
Does the quantity set to 1 represent creating/placing an object, or adjusting an existing stack? The intent is inferred but not explicit.

main_function.name: U8P_TROWEL::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the 'U8P_TROWEL::look' (examine) event for the trowel by preparing minimal item info and invoking the item bark routine to present the string "trowel " to the viewer/process.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_TROWEL::look) — triggered when the player or actor examines/looks at the trowel

function_map.name: U8P_TROWEL::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_TROWEL::look' event: display item name/description via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_TROWEL::use
function_map.role: Handle 'use' event: prompt for target, validate reachability and type, gate with FREE::24A7, then mutate target (quantity/status) and update world state.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine to display/announce text attributed to an item/process (used for both the name and error messages).

function_map.name: U8P_TROWEL::process target()
function_map.role: Engine prompt/selection routine that returns the chosen target id or 0 if none/cancelled.

external_calls.purpose: Present the static string "trowel " to the triggering process/viewer.
Prompt/obtain the target for the use action; loop until a non-zero target is returned.
Verify that the actor/process can reach the chosen target; if not reachable then display "can't reach " and abort.
Read the target's X coordinate (stored for potential context/logic).
Read the target's Y coordinate (stored for potential context/logic).
Obtain the numeric type of the target and accept only types 3 or 4; other types abort processing.

A gating precondition/resource check invoked with small byte parameters; code proceeds only on the expected success return. Exact semantics are uncertain (likely inventory/permission/consumption check).
Set the chosen target's quantity to 1 after FREE::24A7 allows the action.
Apply bitwise OR with mask 0x0080 to the target's status flags (marking it with that state).
Force the target to update/fall so the game world reflects the mutation (refresh stacking/position).
On unreachable target, display the message "can't reach " to the actor/process and return.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements two item event handlers for a trowel: the 'U8P_TROWEL::look' handler (U8P_TROWEL::look) and a 'use' handler (U8P_TROWEL::use).
